authority_boundary: Eval Forge archetypes route design choices only. They do not accept proof, score evidence, create verdicts, promote candidates, or mutate repo-local ports.

archetypes.label: Deterministic validator
use_when: A stable file, schema, topology, manifest, or command contract can be checked without model judgment.
do_not_use_when: The behavior depends on subjective quality, multi-turn trajectory, or ambiguous human preference.
archetypes.fixture_shape: source files or JSON/YAML fixtures with expected structural invariants
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include at least one passing source shape and one violating shape when adding tests.
repeatability_requirements: no network or live runtime dependency
anti_gaming_risks: validator checks wording but misses owner meaning
generated readmodel is treated as proof
archetypes.validation_route: run the owning validator or validation lane command

archetypes.label: Unit or contract test
use_when: The expected behavior is a bounded contract between modules, ports, or source surfaces.
do_not_use_when: The issue is a broad proof claim or a live agent trajectory that cannot be reduced to a component boundary.
archetypes.fixture_shape: small input/output examples or temporary repo trees
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Test both contract satisfaction and a boundary violation.
anti_gaming_risks: test mirrors implementation instead of contract
archetypes.validation_route: run focused pytest for the owning test surface

archetypes.label: Property or invariant test
use_when: Correctness depends on a stable rule across many inputs, states, or route combinations.
do_not_use_when: Only one historically interesting example exists and no durable invariant can be named.
required_owner_surface: nearest source contract or validator route
archetypes.fixture_shape: generated or enumerated cases covering allowed variation
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include valid variation and at least one falsifying counterexample.
repeatability_requirements: same seed or deterministic case enumeration
archetypes.validation_route: run property-focused tests or owning validator

archetypes.label: Trace or trajectory eval
use_when: The final answer may look acceptable but the route, tool sequence, handoff, or mutation order matters.
do_not_use_when: A final response check or static validator fully captures the claim.
archetypes.fixture_shape: messages, tool calls, command spans, state observations, and expected route markers
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include a valid alternative path and a route-breaking path so the eval is not brittle.
repeatability_requirements: path evidence separated from final text
anti_gaming_risks: forcing one exact path when several are valid
judge-only grading hides deterministic route law

archetypes.label: Tool correctness eval
use_when: The key claim is whether the agent chose the right tool and passed valid arguments or results.
do_not_use_when: Tool choice is irrelevant to user-visible correctness or several tool paths are equally valid.
archetypes.grader_family: tool-call exact/subset/superset matcher or calibrated tool rubric
archetypes.fixture_shape: input, expected tool calls, actual tool calls, argument match mode, optional tool outputs
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include cases where a tool should be called and cases where calling it would be overreach.
archetypes.validation_route: run tool trajectory or harness-specific eval command

archetypes.label: State-change eval
use_when: The agent claims completion but the filesystem, database, calendar, repo state, or generated artifact must prove it.
do_not_use_when: The task is read-only or final text is the whole product.
archetypes.fixture_shape: disposable workspace or bounded state snapshot plus verifier command
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include both correct side effect and plausible false-done final response.
anti_gaming_risks: checking text claim instead of state
mutating live state during eval without sandbox
archetypes.validation_route: run state verifier in disposable workspace or check mode

archetypes.label: Final-response eval
use_when: The final response itself is the deliverable and can be judged against explicit content, format, or citation criteria.
do_not_use_when: Tool route, state change, or hidden evidence trail matters more than the final text.
archetypes.fixture_shape: input prompt plus expected answer, schema, or rubric criteria
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include compliant and non-compliant response examples.

local_vs_central_route.owner_repo: app or repo owning the response contract

archetypes.validation_route: run response-schema or rubric eval command

archetypes.label: Freshness or runtime eval
use_when: The route depends on live state, runtime status, mirror freshness, sibling drift, or generated readmodel currency.
do_not_use_when: All source inputs are static and current enough for the claim.
archetypes.fixture_shape: status payloads, timestamps, selected roots, and stale/current examples
repeatability_requirements: volatile live checks separated from source truth
anti_gaming_risks: stale live state ignored because tests pass

archetypes.label: Owner-boundary or proof-readout eval
use_when: The risk is treating a candidate, generated reader, MCP packet, dashboard, or local port as stronger proof than it is.
do_not_use_when: The owner boundary is already enforced by a simple local validator and no proof interpretation changed.
archetypes.fixture_shape: surface ref, claimed meaning, allowed meaning, forbidden meaning
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include valid readmodel use and over-strong proof claim.
anti_gaming_risks: adding broad doctrine instead of fixing route

archetypes.label: Repo-local intake pressure packet
use_when: A repo has concrete eval pressure that should stay local until owner review and central overlap checks happen.
do_not_use_when: The item is ready for central proof or is already covered by an existing local suite.
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include one local pressure item and one duplicate/too-vague item.
repeatability_requirements: central overlap can be checked later

archetypes.label: Repo-local runnable suite
use_when: A repo has a local_eval_suite_execution_v1 sidecar whose aggregate execution state is source-contract-ready.
do_not_use_when: Only a .suite.md note exists, the execution sidecar is absent, invalid, or stale, or the suite claims central verdict, scoring, or baseline authority.
required_evidence: local suite note and matching execution sidecar paths
canonical owner identity verified across PORT and Git common-dir/origin when available
schema-valid typed python_pytest argv and repo-relative cwd
entrypoint and tracked file/tree SHA256 refs
auto-run, runtime-reproducibility, proof-authority, and promotion flags fixed false
JIT revalidation, environment capture, and execution receipt flags fixed true
archetypes.grader_family: local runnable check or regression smoke
archetypes.fixture_shape: repo-local cases plus evals/suites/<slug>.suite.json execution sidecar and bounded report
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include expected pass/fail or regression/no-regression cases.
repeatability_requirements: suite execution state is source-contract-ready after tracked-source hash validation
only the repo owner or aoa-eval-apply JIT-revalidates and invokes runner.argv exactly
owner/apply captures interpreter, dependency, platform, sidecar digest, observed hashes, and an execution receipt
inventory, Eval Forge, readiness, dashboard, session-start, promotion review, and MCP do not execute the suite
report does not claim central proof or pinned runtime reproducibility
anti_gaming_risks: local pass treated as central proof
suite silently depends on stale local state or ambient dependencies
worktree basename used to redefine repo owner
untyped interpreter or module dispatcher bypasses the reviewed entrypoint
archetypes.validation_route: python scripts/validate_local_eval_port.py --target-root <repo>; owner/apply revalidates immediately before exact argv, captures environment, and writes an execution receipt only when state is source-contract-ready

archetypes.label: aoa-skills trigger eval
use_when: The failure is that a skill should have triggered, routed, or stayed manual but did not.
do_not_use_when: The issue is proof meaning or runtime state rather than skill discovery/adoption.
required_evidence: source skill and resolved capability contract
catalog/profile projection and prompt-visible runtime posture
manual positive and negative applicability cases
manual no-skill and coexistence comparison with artifact inspection
required_owner_surface: aoa-skills capability catalog and admitted lifecycle review
archetypes.grader_family: owner-reviewed manual routing and outcome comparison
archetypes.fixture_shape: session-local prompt/case, expected route, acceptance criteria, source/profile/runtime posture, and inspected artifact
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include prompt cases that should trigger and cases that should not.
repeatability_requirements: source commit, model, host, tools, and context boundary known
profile, catalog, and projection state known
manual artifacts and acceptance criteria inspectable
anti_gaming_risks: aoa-evals tries to own skill runtime
archetypes.validation_route: run owner-reviewed manual no-skill, positive, negative, and coexistence trials; admit a durable harness only after a repeated stable invariant

archetypes.label: abyss-stack runtime or MCP smoke
use_when: The claim depends on a live MCP/runtime service, selected root, mirror, or exported runtime candidate.
do_not_use_when: The behavior is only source proof or local repo intake without live runtime dependency.
archetypes.fixture_shape: runtime status JSON, mirror/current examples, candidate export summaries
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include available/current and unavailable/stale runtime states.
repeatability_requirements: live checks separated from release proof
anti_gaming_risks: runtime unavailable blocks unrelated source work
archetypes.validation_route: run aoa-evals-mcp runtime-status or smoke from abyss-stack owner route

archetypes.label: Human-review rubric
use_when: The behavior is important but subjective enough that humans must first define pass/fail criteria.
do_not_use_when: A deterministic validator or route check can decide the case without human calibration.
required_owner_surface: domain owner guide or review packet
archetypes.grader_family: manual rubric with later calibration option
archetypes.fixture_shape: review worksheet, pass/fail examples, disagreement notes
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include edge cases and rejected examples, not only good examples.
anti_gaming_risks: human labels are replaced by judge before calibration
archetypes.validation_route: manual owner review plus schema validation of worksheet/candidate packet

archetypes.label: Calibrated LLM-judge eval
use_when: A rubric is subjective but enough human-labeled examples exist to calibrate a model judge.
do_not_use_when: No human labels, rubric examples, or judge failure analysis exist.
archetypes.fixture_shape: inputs, outputs/traces, rubric, human labels, judge choices
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include pass, fail, borderline, and invalid judge-output examples.
archetypes.validation_route: run judge parser tests and compare against human labels

archetypes.label: Central aoa-evals proof bundle draft
use_when: A recurring, owner-clear, non-duplicate proof question should become a central source eval bundle.
do_not_use_when: The case is only local pressure, candidate evidence, runtime telemetry, or missing human criteria.
archetypes.grader_family: central proof bundle review and report contract
archetypes.fixture_shape: EVAL.md, eval.yaml, origin need, integrity check, optional fixture/runner/report contracts
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Bundle must name cases or fixture contracts before promotion beyond draft.
anti_gaming_risks: duplicate bundle created for existing eval

archetypes.label: Comparison or fixed-baseline eval
use_when: The claim compares a candidate against a named baseline, previous version, or peer under matched conditions.
do_not_use_when: No baseline target, matched fixture family, or comparison rubric exists.
archetypes.fixture_shape: paired baseline/candidate cases with per-case comparison notes
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include candidate stronger, baseline stronger, noisy variation, and not-reviewable examples.
repeatability_requirements: same cases and rubric for both sides
anti_gaming_risks: resource tradeoff hidden as quality improvement

archetypes.label: Longitudinal or window eval
use_when: The claim depends on repeated windows, growth snapshots, recovery patterns, or behavior over time.
do_not_use_when: A single fixed task or one-off regression case is enough.
required_owner_surface: longitudinal eval bundle or comparison-spine window surface
archetypes.fixture_shape: repeated case windows with consistent readout fields
archetypes.positive_negative_case_expectation: Include improved, regressed, unchanged, and not-comparable windows.
anti_gaming_risks: growth claim hides changed task distribution
